items.question_text: Complete: This order applies to all ATO _____ and anyone using ATO directives.
items.explanation: Per 1-1-2, the order applies to all ATO personnel and anyone using ATO directives.

choices.text: personnel

items.question_text: Under 1-1-2, which of the following is true about who this order applies to?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-2, the order applies to all ATO personnel and anyone using ATO directives. A contractor who relies on ATO directives falls under the second category.

choices.text: Only personnel who hold an active ATC certification.
Only FAA employees, not contractors or military personnel.
All ATO personnel and anyone using ATO directives, regardless of employment status.
Only facility managers and supervisors, not line controllers.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-3, which two websites make this order available online?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-3, the order is available on the FAA Air Traffic Plans and Publications website and the FAA Orders and Notices website.

choices.text: The MyFAA employee portal and the FAA Orders and Notices website.
The FAA Air Traffic Plans and Publications website and the FAA Orders and Notices website.
The FAA Air Traffic Plans and Publications website and the GovDelivery subscription portal.
The FAA Orders and Notices website and the AJV-P correspondence mailbox.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-3, the order is available on the FAA's Air Traffic Plans and Publications website and the FAA _____ website.
items.explanation: Per 1-1-3, the second website is the FAA Orders and Notices website.

choices.text: Orders and Notices

items.question_text: A user claims this order can be found on the MyFAA employee portal but not on any public website. Is that correct under 1-1-3?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-3, the order is available on two public FAA websites: the Air Traffic Plans and Publications website and the Orders and Notices website. The MyFAA portal hosts interpretations (per 1-1-9g), not the order itself.

choices.text: Yes, the order is only available on the MyFAA portal.
No; the order is available on two FAA public websites, not exclusively on MyFAA.
Yes, and it is also only available in paper form at regional offices.
No; the order is available only through the GovDelivery subscription system.

items.question_text: Complete: The significant changes to this order are identified in the _____ page(s).
items.explanation: Per 1-1-5, significant changes are identified in the Explanation of Changes pages.

choices.text: Explanation of Changes

items.question_text: Under 1-1-5, what is the difference between where changes are identified and where the full order text lives?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-5, significant changes are identified in the Explanation of Changes pages, which are separate from the body of the order itself. Retaining these pages is advisable throughout the duration of the basic order.

choices.text: Changes are embedded inline in each affected paragraph with no separate summary.
Changes are identified in the Explanation of Changes pages, distinct from the order body, and should be retained throughout the basic order's life.
Changes are listed in the table of contents only and discarded after reading.
Changes are announced via email and never documented in the order itself.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-6, this order and its changes are scheduled to be published to coincide with what dates?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-6a, the order and its changes are scheduled to coincide with AIRAC dates.

choices.text: The first business day of each quarter.
AIRAC dates.
The publication date of each NOTAM cycle.
The anniversary of the order's original issue date.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-6, what must a change initiator do to meet the publication effective date?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-6b, change initiators must submit proposed changes well in advance of the cutoff date because review and coordination often take several months.

choices.text: Submit the proposed change on the cutoff date itself; the review is automatic.
Submit well in advance of the cutoff date because review and coordination often take several months.
Submit after the cutoff date; late submissions are always accepted.
Submit any time before the effective date; the cutoff date only applies to printing.

items.question_text: Complete: Under 1-1-6, the cutoff date for completion is the deadline for a proposed change to be fully _____ and signed.
items.explanation: Per 1-1-6b, the cutoff date is the deadline for a proposed change to be fully coordinated and signed.

choices.text: coordinated

items.question_text: Under 1-1-7, what is the common time threshold that applies to website availability, FAA facility notification, and military facility notification?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-7, all three provisions (website availability, FAA notification, military notification) use the same 30-day threshold before the effective date.

choices.text: 14 days before the effective date.
60 days before the effective date.
30 days before the effective date.
45 days before the effective date.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-7, if a military facility has not received the order or changes at least 30 days before the effective date, what must it do and which table provides the contact information?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-7c, the facility must notify its appropriate military headquarters, and TBL 1-1-2 provides the Military Distribution Contacts.

choices.text: Notify its service area office distribution officer using TBL 1-1-1.
Notify AJV-P directly using the GovDelivery subscription link.
Notify its appropriate military headquarters using TBL 1-1-2.
Notify TBL 1-1-3 Military Operations Interface Offices using DSN.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-8d, when will procedural changes be made to this order?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-8d, procedural changes will not be made until the operational system software has been adapted to accomplish the revised procedures.

choices.text: As soon as AJV-P approves the proposed language, regardless of software readiness.
After the operational system software has been adapted to accomplish the revised procedures.
Immediately upon the next AIRAC date following coordination.
Only during a complete order reissue, never during a change publication.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-8, which FAA order contains additional details on the submission process and AJV-P processing responsibilities?
items.explanation: The note in 1-1-8 references FAA Order JO 7000.5 for details on submission process and AJV-P processing responsibilities.

choices.text: FAA Order JO 7210.3, Facility Operation and Administration.
FAA Order JO 7000.5, Procedures for Submitting Changes to Air Traffic Control Publications.
FAA Order JO 1000.37, Air Traffic Organization Safety Management System.
FAA Order JO 7110.10, Flight Services.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-13, which examples are given of FAA regional office organizations that are not part of the Air Traffic Organization?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-13, the paragraph lists Communications Center, Flight Standards, and Airport offices as examples of non-ATO regional office organizations.

choices.text: Communications Center, Flight Standards, and Airport offices.
Technical Operations, Safety, and Training offices.
Air Traffic Services, System Operations, and Mission Support offices.
AJV-P, AJI, and AJT offices.

items.situation: A contractor performing work for the ATO is relying on ATO directives but claims the order does not apply because they are not an FAA employee.
items.question_text: Does 1-1-2 limit applicability to only FAA employees?

choices.text: No; it applies to all ATO personnel and anyone using ATO directives, regardless of employment status.
Yes; only direct FAA employees are covered by the order.
Yes; contractors must follow their own company manuals instead.
Only if the contractor's contract explicitly references the order.

items.explanation: Per 1-1-2, the order applies to all ATO personnel and anyone using ATO directives. The second category captures anyone who relies on ATO directives, regardless of their employment relationship.

items.source_label: FAA Air Traffic Plans and Publications website
lookup_context: A user needs to find the current text of FAA Order JO 7110.65 online
items.task: Identify which FAA websites host the order text.
items.question_text: A controller needs to pull up the current JO 7110.65 text online. Which websites does 1-1-3 identify as hosting the order?

choices.text: The FAA Air Traffic Plans and Publications website, and the FAA Orders and Notices website.
Only the GovDelivery subscription portal.
Only the MyFAA employee portal.
Only the AJV-P correspondence mailbox archive.

items.explanation: Per 1-1-3, the order is available on two public FAA websites: Air Traffic Plans and Publications, and Orders and Notices.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-5, how long should the Explanation of Changes pages be retained?

choices.text: Throughout the duration of the basic order.
Only until the next change is published.
They should be discarded after the first reading.
Only if a local supplement references them.

items.explanation: Per 1-1-5, it is advisable to retain the Explanation of Changes pages throughout the duration of the basic order.

lookup_context: A change initiator needs to know the coordination and publication timeline for JO 7110.65CC
items.task: Use TBL 1-1-1 to identify the cutoff and effective dates for JO 7110.65CC.
items.question_text: For the next basic edition JO 7110.65CC, what are the cutoff date for completion and the effective date of publication according to TBL 1-1-1?

choices.text: Cutoff July 9, 2026; effective December 24, 2026.
Cutoff December 24, 2026; effective June 10, 2027.
Cutoff January 22, 2026; effective July 9, 2026.
Cutoff and effective are the same date: December 24, 2026.

items.explanation: TBL 1-1-1 shows JO 7110.65CC cutoff date July 9, 2026 and effective date December 24, 2026.

items.situation: A military facility using the order discovers 20 days before the effective date that it has not received the change package. Someone suggests waiting until after the effective date to report it, since the order will be available on the FAA website anyway.
items.question_text: What does 1-1-7 require the military facility to do in this situation?

choices.text: Notify its appropriate military headquarters, using TBL 1-1-2 for contact information.
Download the order from the FAA website and take no further action.
Wait until the effective date passes, then report the late receipt.
Contact the nearest FAA service area office distribution officer.

items.explanation: Per 1-1-7c, military facilities that have not received the order at least 30 days before the effective date must notify their appropriate military headquarters. TBL 1-1-2 lists the contacts.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-8d, a proposed procedural change is fully coordinated and signed. The operational system software has not yet been updated to support the new procedure. Can the change be published in the order?

choices.text: No; procedural changes will not be made until the operational system software has been adapted.
Yes; once coordinated and signed, the change can be published immediately.
Yes, but only if a waiver under 1-1-11 has also been approved.
Yes; software adaptation is advisory only and does not block publication.

items.explanation: Per 1-1-8d, procedural changes will not be made to the order until the operational system software has been adapted to accomplish the revised procedures.
